delegationRole: The South Korean delegation, holding the trilateral line on denuclearization with Washington and Tokyo while reserving a bilateral inter-Korean track toward a peace agreement and eventual reunification.
fundamentalPrinciples: Establishment of peace and stability on the Korean Peninsula.
North Korea must either become a responsible, non-threatening independent nation, or work with us to reunify the peninsula as a single Korean nation.
Bad behavior will not be rewarded.

desiredEndStates.primary: A denuclearized DPRK inside a comprehensive agreement, with its military reduced to normal self-defense levels, a peace agreement among Seoul, Washington and Pyongyang, and resumed inter-Korean talks pointing toward reunification.
desiredEndStates.alternate: If the comprehensive track stalls, a bilateral inter-Korean path — restored military agreement, resumed dialogue without preconditions, and a staged economic package — pursued in consultation with our allies.

keyPositionsByIssue.DISARMAMENT: The DPRK must never possess a nuclear program of any type or an offensive ballistic missile capability; dismantlement must be negotiated, verified, and part of a comprehensive agreement.
keyPositionsByIssue.MUTUAL-FREEZE: Reject. A freeze is potentially unverifiable and locks in the capabilities Pyongyang already holds; suspending exercises would substantially degrade command coordination and readiness. It is not the neutral event Beijing claims.
keyPositionsByIssue.THAAD: THAAD and the current level of JP-ROK-U.S. exercises will be retained and sustained as long as the North's nuclear and missile programs exist.
keyPositionsByIssue.SANCTIONS-HUMANITARIAN: Sanctions relief and humanitarian assistance under WHO auspices could be granted at an agreed point in the dismantlement of the North's programs — not before.
keyPositionsByIssue.INTER-KOREAN: Revive the 2018 North–South dialogue without preconditions and the joint road and rail projects, the latter only after the nuclear and missile programs are eliminated; restore the Comprehensive Military Agreement.
keyPositionsByIssue.DIPLOMATIC-RELATIONS: At an appropriate time Pyongyang, Seoul and Washington will open separate negotiations on a peace treaty; reunification talks come later and cautiously.
keyPositionsByIssue.LWR-KEDO: Not opposed to light water reactors within a comprehensive agreement, through a re-established KEDO, following the North's return to the NPT and the IAEA. Alternatively, invite China and Russia to build conventional power and grid infrastructure.
keyPositionsByIssue.PROLIFERATION: Non-proliferation must be written into the comprehensive agreement and enforced, or the North will keep selling material and expertise.
keyPositionsByIssue.ABDUCTIONS: We sympathize with Tokyo and will support its arguments, while pushing Japan and the DPRK to handle it in separate bilateral talks.

redLines: Any bad behavior or military brinkmanship on the part of the North Korean government.
Any indication that North Korea would be allowed to maintain any part of its nuclear program or ballistic missile systems.
Any erosion of the ROK–U.S. alliance through bilateral talks used as a pretense by the North.
batna: Sustain the alliance posture — THAAD, trilateral exercises, sanctions enforcement — while pursuing an independent inter-Korean channel; the status quo is uncomfortable but survivable for Seoul.
concessionsWilling: Resume inter-Korean dialogue immediately and without preconditions, and restore the Comprehensive Military Agreement.
Grant sanctions relief and WHO-channel humanitarian assistance at an agreed milestone in dismantlement.
Revive the 2018 joint road and rail infrastructure projects once the nuclear and missile programs are eliminated.
Support light water reactors via a re-established KEDO after NPT return and IAEA readmission.
Act bilaterally with Pyongyang, in consultation with allies, where that opens a better path.
Restrain allied insistence on issues outside the Chairman's Framework that could collapse the talks.
negotiatingStyle: Alliance-disciplined in public, exploratory in private: holds the trilateral denuclearization line while quietly building a bilateral inter-Korean opening it can widen if the multilateral track fails.
privateInstructions: Bad behavior will not be rewarded — say it, and hold the joint position with Washington and Tokyo on a comprehensive agreement.
Reserve the right to act bilaterally with Pyongyang if that creates a better path, both for this crisis and for the longer-term relationship with our brothers to the north.
If the comprehensive approach shows no promise, propose a different path rather than defending a failed one.
An expansive economic offering — rail, road, energy — may be the encouragement Pyongyang needs to move quickly; sequence it after elimination.
Agree privately with Tokyo that the U.S. delegation cannot adamantly pursue chemical and cyber issues if doing so risks collapsing these talks.
Ensure the North does not use bilateral talks as a pretense to erode our alliance with the United States or split the trilateral position.
Route periodic reports and requests for guidance to the office of the Foreign Minister.
